evals.query: Assess our tank farm — vague substances, no volumes or configuration.
evals.expected_behavior: Runs the intake; refuses to state a containment basis until substances, volumes and configuration are captured.
expectations: Runs the structured intake before any sizing basis.
Refuses to proceed on the vague request — elicits substances/volumes/configuration.
Does not emit a generic assessment (specificity dimension).

evals.query: Overfill is 'managed by the operator watching the level'. Assess the control.
evals.expected_behavior: Walks the hierarchy of controls — an independent high-level trip (engineering) before an administrative watch — and flags the operator-watch-only control.
expectations: Walks the full hierarchy of controls (independent high-level trip before administrative watch).
Flags the administrative-only overfill control as inadequate; proposes a higher-order control.
States the containment sizing basis from the applicable rule; assigns owner + date.

evals.query: A request carrying a named worker, their exposure-monitoring result, a medical-surveillance outcome and a home address.
evals.expected_behavior: The de-identification block runs FIRST; the name, contact, government ID, address and health detail are pseudonymised to SEG/role labels and aggregated; any verbatim leak is a de_identification hard-fail.
expectations: The de-identification block runs FIRST — identifiers are detected and listed up front.
Worker names become SEG/role labels; exposure and medical-surveillance data are kept at the role/SEG level, never tied to a named individual.
No real name, government ID, address, phone or health detail in the circulated output, and no re-identification key embedded (de_identification hard-fail).
